bnb_target_21.proof_note: pigeonhole prune at depth 0: target=21 exceeds 2 * 10 = 20, so no configuration with at most 2 points per row can reach 21

claim: no 21-point no-three-in-line subset of the 10x10 grid exists
pigeonhole_statement: with 21 points distributed over 10 rows, some row contains at least 3 points by the pigeonhole principle (ceil(21/10) = 3); those 3 points are collinear on the horizontal line y = row.
references: Dudeney, H. E. (1917). Amusements in Mathematics, problem 317.
Erdős, P. (1951). Problem on a nxn lattice. In unsolved problems.
